title: Asynchronous Architecture and Pattern Selection
description: A request path adds a broker without a latency or failure reason, hides synchronous coupling behind RPC messages, and uses one global queue for work distribution, broadcasting, and replay. Learners cumulatively produce pattern selection and coupling decision record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value.
Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility.
Evaluate request/reply over messaging against ordinary HTTP using correlation, timeout, cancellation, failure ambiguity, and capacity evidence.
